body.title: Anticipation slice 3 — basal_ganglia / action selection reads bus.top_k to bias toward high-want
body.result: Slice 3 shipped. anticipator.anticipation_bias_for_referent helper + basal_ganglia step 2c bias-application for pursuit_id-tagged habits. IGOR_ANTICIPATION_BIAS_WEIGHT (default 0.1). Logs INFO when bias actually moves selection. 10 new tests. Suite 4347 → 4357. No-op for current habits but loop closed structurally — moment any habit gets pursuit_id metadata, BG bias kicks in.
body.description:
## Akien pre-approval — THIRD time given (2026-05-03)

CC has flagged this as HIGH-inertia-needs-pre-approval three separate times now. Akien has approved each time. Reading this back: the inertia gate is satisfied; sprint without re-asking. Remove `gate` field; greenlight stands.

---

Slice 3 of T-anticipation-primitive (slice 1 substrate shipped 2026-05-02 commit 6819d2a5; slice 2 = pursuit emit, separate ticket). Consumer-side wiring: action selection reads AnticipationBus.top_k() and biases decision toward high-want referents.

**Goal:** the loop closes — Igor's pull-signal influences action selection, not just sits in a bus passively.

**Mechanism (sketch — exact integration point TBD at sprint):**
```python
from wild_igor.igor.cognition.anticipator import get_bus

# In basal_ganglia / TWM consumer / reasoner-depth selection:
top_anticipations = get_bus().top_k(k=5)
# Use top_anticipations[].predicted_delta * confidence as bias signal
# alongside existing priority/boredom signals
```

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/basal_ganglia.py (HIGH-inertia) + possibly cognition/turn_pipeline.py + tests/test_basal_ganglia_anticipation_bias.py (NEW).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code; oop-first; HIGH-inertia-pre-approval (basal_ganglia.py); intentionally weaker than priority/boredom (anticipation is directional but not overriding — same calibration intuition the existing closure-valence helper uses with IGOR_ANTICIPATION_WEIGHT=0.3).

**Scope boundary:** IN — one concrete plug-point in action selection, weighted bias signal. OUT — milieu/RPE feedback (slice 4); rewriting basal_ganglia rule structure.

**Test plan:** unit test that pushing a high-predicted_delta anticipation shifts action selection toward the matching referent in a controlled basket; regression test that an empty bus leaves selection identical to today's behavior.

**Related:** T-anticipation-primitive (parent); T-anticipation-slice2-pursuit-emit (provides the producers); T-anticipation-pull (existing closure-valence helper, may consolidate into this primitive in a later slice).
